  "findings": [
    "Fall protection trigger height of 6 feet was ignored, leaving workers exposed to fall hazards without personal fall arrest systems, guardrails, or safety nets."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately in the affected area.",
    "Install compliant fall protection systems (guardrails, safety nets, or personal fall arrest systems) before allowing work to resume."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under OSHA 1926 Subpart M, fall protection is strictly required once the fall distance reaches or exceeds 6 feet in construction. Ignoring this trigger height creates an immediate, critical safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M (1926.501)",
    "OSHA 1910.140"
  ]
}
